summary: Fully decompiled and linked the Tosakinto item module by replacing the nonmatching object entry with Matching, adding item-specific vars and attrs, filling out the item state table and motion callbacks, correcting prototypes, and wiring the Tosakinto item vars into the Item union.
what_changed: Changed configure.py so melee/it/items/ittosakinto.c builds as Object(Matching) instead of Object(NonMatching).
Added itTosakinto_ItemVars to itCommonItems.h with a 0x60-byte pad followed by x60_msid, x64_vel_x, and x68_sfx_id fields mapped to ip+E34, ip+E38, and ip+E3C.
Added itTosakinto_Attrs containing x0_scale, x4_life_timer, and x8_vel_x for article special attributes.
Added itTosakinto_ItemVars tosakinto to the Item xDD4_itemVar union in src/melee/it/types.h.
Expanded ittosakinto.c from a small partial implementation into a full matched module, including includes for platform, random, stage, item inlines, item common definitions, and lb audio.
Defined GET_ATTRS(it) as a local cast of xC4_article_data->x4_specialAttributes to itTosakinto_Attrs.
Added ItemStateTable it_803F7A48 with five state entries using Tosakinto motion 2, 3, and 4 anim/phys/coll callbacks.
Implemented setup and transition helpers including it_802C8F4C, it_802C8FE4, it_802C90E8, it_802C93BC, and it_802C9468.
Implemented motion callbacks for Tosakinto motion 2, including life timer countdown in Anim, horizontal velocity application in Phys, and collision-driven state transitions in Coll.
Adjusted itTosakinto_UnkMotion4_Coll to return bool from it_8027A118 instead of being void.
Implemented itTosakinto_UnkMotion3_Coll as a bool callback that calls it_8026E15C with it_802C90E8 and returns false.
Implemented it_2725_Logic0_Destroyed with a bottom blast-zone check and conditional lbAudioAx_800263E8 call using the saved Tosakinto SFX id.
Updated ittosakinto.h prototypes to use Item_GObj consistently, expose the newly implemented functions, and correct bool-returning collision callbacks.
smart_moves: Introduced item-specific vars in the shared Item xDD4_itemVar union instead of using raw offsets throughout the C implementation.

Separated persistent runtime state from article attributes: x60_msid, x64_vel_x, and x68_sfx_id live in itTosakinto_ItemVars, while scale, life timer, and base horizontal velocity live in itTosakinto_Attrs.
Used a small GET_ATTRS macro to keep repeated specialAttributes casts readable while preserving the low-level article-data access pattern.
Matched state-machine behavior by populating the ItemStateTable with explicit animation IDs and callback triplets rather than leaving behavior implicit.
Preserved matching-sensitive stack behavior with PAD_STACK(0x8) in functions where the decompiled shape required it.
Handled random left/right movement by assigning attrs->x8_vel_x and negating it based on HSD_Randi(2), rather than baking directional constants into multiple branches.
Saved it->xD6C into the Tosakinto item var x68_sfx_id after setup, then reused that stored id in the destroyed callback for audio cleanup/continuation behavior.
Corrected header signatures to reflect actual callback return types, especially collision callbacks that must return bool.
decomp_lessons: When linking a previously nonmatching item module, update configure.py only after the source, headers, item vars, and callback signatures are complete enough to build as Matching.
For item modules using xDD4_itemVar, add a named union member in it/types.h and define the corresponding item-var struct in itCommonItems.h; this makes later matching work safer and more searchable.
Article special attributes should be represented as a dedicated attrs struct when multiple constants are read from xC4_article_data->x4_specialAttributes.
Item motion collision callbacks may need bool return types even when the previous stub or prototype was void; match the caller/table expectations and propagate helper return values where required.
State transition helpers such as Item_80268E5C calls should encode the target state IDs and ITEM_ANIM_UPDATE behavior directly when that is what the original code does.
Matching sometimes requires preserving apparently artificial local variables, temporary bools, and PAD_STACK calls to reproduce codegen.
Bottom blast-zone comparisons can matter in destroyed callbacks; here the code checks pos.y - 50.0F against Stage_GetBlastZoneBottomOffset before playing audio.
naming_conventions: Item-specific structs use the pattern itTosakinto_ItemVars and itTosakinto_Attrs.
Item-var union members use the lowercase item name, here xDD4_itemVar.tosakinto.
Unknown decompiled callbacks retain address-based names or motion-placeholder names such as it_802C90E8 and itTosakinto_UnkMotion2_Anim.
Motion callback names follow the existing item convention it<ItemName>_UnkMotionN_Anim, _Phys, and _Coll.
Struct fields preserve offset-oriented names such as x60_msid, x64_vel_x, x68_sfx_id, x0_scale, x4_life_timer, and x8_vel_x.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Used PAD_STACK(0x8) in it_802C90E8 and itTosakinto_UnkMotion2_Coll, indicating stack-padding was needed for matching.
Kept an intermediate bool temp in itTosakinto_UnkMotion2_Coll before branching on it_8026DA08 and it_80272C6C, likely preserving original control-flow/codegen shape.
Assigned Vec3 scale components individually in z, y, x order before HSD_JObjSetScale, which may reflect matching-sensitive instruction ordering.
Used explicit unsigned constants in Item_8026AE84, including 0x7FU and 0x40U, preserving expected argument types/codegen.
Wrapped the special-attributes cast in GET_ATTRS but still reads through xC4_article_data->x4_specialAttributes, matching the original data path.
Returned the result of it_8027A118 directly from itTosakinto_UnkMotion4_Coll after correcting the prototype to bool.
review_feedback: No review comments were present in the provided PR slice.
No PR body text was available in the provided context, so rationale beyond the title and diff is inferred only from code changes.
follow_up_queries: Find other Pokémon item PRs that add item-specific xDD4_itemVar structs and move modules from NonMatching to Matching.
Search for other item collision callbacks corrected from void to bool during matching work.
Compare Tosakinto state table patterns with itchicorita, itchicoritaleaf, and itkabigon item modules.
Look for other uses of PAD_STACK(0x8) in item motion callbacks to identify common matching tactics.
Search for destroyed callbacks that check Stage_GetBlastZoneBottomOffset before calling lbAudioAx_800263E8.
